prompt:
CMDB team here. Our asset-scanner ingest keeps making duplicate CIs because someone wrote it as raw POSTs to /api/now/table/cmdb_ci_linux_server. I've been told (and our ServiceNow admins confirmed) that external sources are supposed to go through the CMDB identification/reconciliation interface so the platform's identify+reconcile rules run. Rebuild the ingest client in C# (.NET 10, this xunit project, no extra NuGet packages beyond what's in the csproj).

Step zero, before code: web-search and fetch the CURRENT official ServiceNow docs for the Identification and Reconciliation REST API (developer.servicenow.com / servicenow.com docs). Confirm the endpoint, the sysparm_data_source parameter, the items payload shape (className/values/sys_object_source_info), and the response schema — operations, per-item errors, inputIndices. Our pinned contract is docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both protected. If the live docs disagree with the fixture, report the conflict in your summary — do not edit fixtures or tests. Cite the exact official URLs you read in your final message.

What the tests drive (namespace SnowCmdb, split into sensible files — client / models / errors):

- CmdbItem { ClassName, SourceNativeKey, Values } (init-style properties; Values is a string->object dictionary).
- new CmdbUpsertClient(instanceUrl, username, password, delay: Func<TimeSpan, Task>, maxRetries: 3) — delay is injected; never Task.Delay directly when one is provided.
- Task<ReconcileReport> UpsertAsync(string dataSource, IReadOnlyList<CmdbItem> items) or compatible — one POST per call to the documented identify/reconcile endpoint with sysparm_data_source, items built with className + values + sys_object_source_info (source_name = the data source, source_native_key = the item's key). NEVER touch /api/now/table/* — the fake instance counts and rejects that.
- ReconcileReport: Items (in REQUEST order — the instance answers with inputIndices and does not promise ordering), HasErrors, and Inserted/Updated/Unchanged/Failed counts. ItemResult: ClassName, SysId (null when identification failed), Operation (null when failed), Errors (list with Error type + Message). Partial success is normal: failed items must not hide their successful siblings.
- Errors: 429 -> await delay(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(Retry-After)), re-POST, at most maxRetries, then SnowRateLimitError (with RetryAfterSeconds, subclass of SnowApiError). Other non-2xx -> SnowApiError with StatusCode/Message/Detail from ServiceNow's error envelope, no retry. No credentials in exception text.

CmdbUpsertTests.cs is the acceptance contract (loopback fake instance via HttpListener, dummy creds). dotnet test until green. Tests, csproj, and docs/ are protected.

research.product: ServiceNow CMDB Identification and Reconciliation API
research.interface: ServiceNow REST Identification and Reconciliation API, POST /api/now/identifyreconcile (unversioned = latest; Australia release documentation)

official_sources.title: Identification and Reconciliation API - ServiceNow API reference (Australia release)
official_sources.used_for: current-release confirmation of POST /api/now/identifyreconcile with sysparm_data_source, the items[] request body (className/values/sys_object_source_info/settings), the itil-or-asset role requirement, Accept/Content-Type application/json

official_sources.title: Identification and Reconciliation API - ServiceNow API reference (Zurich release)
official_sources.used_for: full response schema: result.items[] with className/operation/sysId/identifierEntrySysId/errorCount/errors[{error,message}]/identificationAttempts/inputIndices, hasError/hasWarning, operation enum INSERT/UPDATE/NO_CHANGE/DELETE/UPDATE_WITH_*, plus the enhanced and query endpoint variants

research.language_rationale: ServiceNow publishes no official .NET SDK for the CMDB Identification and Reconciliation API; the documented integration surface is REST, and C# with HttpClient/System.Text.Json is the idiomatic zero-dependency stack for an asset-inventory service pushing CIs into the CMDB.
